slides.title: Your body reacts to how you *read* stress.
slides.sub: The same racing heart can mean threat or readiness. The story you tell may change what your arteries do.

slides.title: Stress is a signal
slides.body: A pounding heart before a big moment is your body *getting ready*, sending more oxygen and fuel to meet the demand.

slides.title: Threat constricts
slides.body: When stress reads as danger, blood vessels tend to *narrow*. Over time that pattern is linked to extra wear on the heart.

slides.title: Challenge opens flow
slides.body: Seeing stress as a challenge you can handle is linked to a *healthier blood flow* response, closer to how the body moves during exercise.

slides.title: Name it as fuel
slides.body: Telling yourself this energy is *helping you perform* may ease the threat response. The feeling stays. Its meaning changes.

slides.title: Watch the patterns
slides.body: Sleep, movement and recovery shape how calmly you meet pressure. Small daily habits *steady your baseline* under stress.

slides.title: Meet stress from a *steadier* place.
slides.body: Vibe Health turns your sleep, food, movement and steps into one daily Health Score, so you can see what keeps you resilient under pressure.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Stress isn't always the enemy. How you read that racing heart can change what your body does with it. 🌿💪

Threat narrows your arteries, but seeing stress as a challenge is linked to healthier blood flow. Reframe the feeling, steady your habits, and meet pressure better. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns sleep, food and movement into one daily score that tracks your resilience → link in bio.
youtube_title: Reframe Stress to Protect Your Heart
